steps.title: Identify the formula needed
steps.content: To find mass, use mass = density × volume. We know density = 0.250 g/cm³, so we must calculate the volume first.

steps.title: Calculate the cube's volume
steps.content: For a cube with edge length 3.000 cm: $$V = (3.000)^3 = 27.000 \text{ cm}^3$$

steps.title: Calculate the mass
steps.content:
Apply the mass formula: $$\text{mass} = 0.250 \text{ g/cm}^3 \times 27.000 \text{ cm}^3 = 6.75 \text{ grams}$$
Equivalent forms accepted: 6.75, 6.750.
